audit_items.proof_trace: nfp_hwmon.c contains no direct kmalloc/kzalloc/vmalloc calls; the only managed resource is the hwmon device registered via hwmon_device_register_with_info() and stored in pf->hwmon_dev. On success, nfp_hwmon_unregister() is always called from __nfp_pci_shutdown() (nfp_main.c:811), which correctly calls hwmon_device_unregister(). On registration failure (ERR_PTR returned), the hwmon subsystem frees its own partial allocations internally and nfp_hwmon_unregister() is never called (probe fails, shutdown callback is not invoked), so there is no leak.
